assumptions: No Spacelift account, source-control integration, cloud integration, stack, teammate invitation, or run is assumed.

developer_goal: Create a Spacelift account and follow the general Quick Start until the first stack run finishes.

first_success.outcome: The first Spacelift stack run finishes and its run view shows the result.
first_success.observable_signal: The run reaches a finished state in the run view after Trigger and Confirm.

route_selection.surface: Spacelift Quick Start through the first stack run
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Spacelift Quick Start through the first stack run
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Spacelift Intent
candidates.reason: It is a separate AI-assisted project surface, while the documentation navigation places Quick Start as the general entry.

candidates.name: Self-Hosted Spacelift
candidates.reason: It is a separate deployment model rather than the hosted Quick Start.

required_path.action: Complete Spacelift account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Spacelift account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Spacelift candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The run reaches a finished state in the run view after Trigger and Confirm.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'A hosted Spacelift account in the selected Europe or United States region is required before the Quick Start screens can be used.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}

{'order': 2, 'type': 'identity', 'requirement': "Signup requires choosing GitHub, GitLab, Google, or Microsoft; the public page does not document any provider's complete authorization and return sequence.", 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'legal', 'requirement': "The free-trial page says signing up signifies agreement to Spacelift's Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.", 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}

external_gates.description: The account region must be selected before signup and later login must use the same region.
All public signup options hand control to an external identity provider whose completion and return are not documented by Spacelift.
The page ties signup to agreement with the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

excluded.item: Fixing the starter repository's purposeful plan-policy issue occurs after the first run.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Policies, contexts, production hardening, self-hosting, migration, and ongoing run operations occur after the intended terminal.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Spacelift show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
